If sin x = 1/2 and x is in the first quadrant, then cos x is:
questions.explanation: Using sin²x + cos²x = 1, cos x = √(1 - sin²x) = √(1 - (1/2)²) = √(3/4) = √3/2. In the first quadrant, cos x is positive.

questions.text: If cos x = -1/2 and x is in the third quadrant, then sin x is:
questions.explanation: In the third quadrant, both sine and cosine are negative. sin x = -√(1 - cos²x) = -√(1 - (-1/2)²) = -√(3/4) = -√3/2.

questions.text: The value of tan(A - B) is:
options: (tan A + tan B) / (1 - tan A tan B)
(tan A - tan B) / (1 + tan A tan B)
(tan A - tan B) / (1 - tan A tan B)
questions.explanation: This is the difference identity for tangent.

questions.text: The value of tan(3π/4) is:
questions.explanation: 3π/4 radians is 135 degrees. tan(135°) = tan(180° - 45°) = -tan(45°) = -1.

questions.text: The domain of the function f(x) = sec x is:
questions.explanation: sec x = 1/cos x. cos x is zero at odd multiples of π/2.
